page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD EPYC 9T24 processors running at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each of the 64 vCPUs maps directly to a dedicated physical core, eliminating resource contention from simultaneous multithreading.

The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external block storage volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance type.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.r8a.16xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads by providing a large RAM footprint relative to the compute allocation. The dedicated core model ensures predictable CPU performance, though the lack of local NVMe storage and GPU acceleration represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local caching or parallelized graphics processing.

This instance configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise resource planning applications, and data analytics pipelines that require substantial memory capacity without requiring specialized GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Powered by AMD EPYC 9T24 processors running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based instance allocates one thread per physical core to ensure dedicated, predictable compute performance. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on external storage solutions and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The high memory density makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads. It is designed for enterprise applications, in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data processing workloads that demand substantial memory capacity without the need for local NVMe drives or hardware accelerators.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T24 vCPUs with a 1:1 physical core mapping
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for reliable data transfer
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
